steps.title: Spot the consumption figure
steps.content: The passage explicitly states the blue whale can consume “as much as 45,000 kg of krill per day”—a figure that directly establishes its consumption scale.

steps.title: Apply this to determine the answer
steps.content:
Choice C correctly concludes the blue whale eats more krill per day, which follows directly from the passage's statement that it can consume 45,000 kg while the minke whale eats only up to 30 percent of its body mass (1,200 kg maximum). Choices A and B contradict the passage, while D invents claims about food scarcity the text never addresses.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
